reasoning: Pope Francis (88) has been hospitalized since February 14, 2025, for double pneumonia, an exceptionally severe illness for an 88-year-old with a partial lung. He has required non-invasive mechanical ventilation at night and high-flow oxygen. As of mid-March, doctors report his prognosis is no longer "guarded" and he is showing slight, gradual improvement (weaning off night ventilation, concelebrating Mass in his suite, releasing a photo). The market was around 74% in early March when his condition was still guarded; with recent improvements, the immediate risk of death has somewhat decreased. Still, the 9-month mortality for an 88-year-old following a 30+ day hospitalization for severe respiratory failure is very high (easily 40-50%). Additionally, he has unsealed a pre-written resignation letter in case of long-term medical impairment, adding perhaps 15-20% probability that he steps down if he survives but remains too frail to govern. Together, these push the total probability into the 60-70% range. I estimate a 64% chance he ceases to be Pope before Jan 1, 2026.
